title: The default namespace should not be used

input_row.title: The default namespace should not be used
input_row.description: Kubernetes provides a default namespace, where objects are placed if no namespace is specified for them. Placing objects in this namespace makes application of RBAC and other controls more difficult.
input_row.rationale: Resources in a Kubernetes cluster should be segregated by namespace, to allow for security controls to be applied at that level and to make it easier to manage resources. Impact: None
input_row.audit: Run this command to list objects in default namespace kubectl get $(kubectl api-resources --verbs=list --namespaced=true -o name | paste -sd, -) --ignore-not-found -n default The only entries there should be system managed resources such as the kubernetes service
input_row.remediation: Ensure that namespaces are created to allow for appropriate segregation of Kubernetes resources and that all new resources are created in a specific namespace. Default Value: Unless a namespace is specific on object creation, the default namespace will be used

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_namespace_default_namespace_usage_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the Kubernetes API to enumerate all namespaced resource types using `kubectl api-resources --verbs=list --namespaced=true -o name`.
- For each namespaced resource type, list all objects in the `default` namespace using:
  ```
  kubectl get <resource> -n default
  ```
  or, to aggregate:
  ```
  kubectl get $(kubectl api-resources --verbs=list --namespaced=true -o name | paste -sd, -) --ignore-not-found -n default
  ```
- Identify all resources present in the `default` namespace.
- Exclude system-managed resources (e.g., the `kubernetes` service of type `Service`).
- Pass Criteria: Only system-managed resources (such as the default `kubernetes` service) exist in the `default` namespace; no user-created Deployments, Pods, Services, ConfigMaps, Secrets, etc. are present.
- Fail Criteria: Any user-created resource (other than the default `kubernetes` service) is found in the `default` namespace.
- The program should output a list of non-system resources found in the `default` namespace for remediation.
